inclusions: Extracts, essences and concentrates of coffee, and preparations with a basis of these or with a basis of coffee (including instant coffee, coffee paste with vegetable fats, coffee preparations with sugar)
Extracts, essences and concentrates of tea or maté, and preparations thereof (including tea extract powder, tea mixed with milk powder and sugar)
Roasted chicory and other roasted coffee substitutes (not containing coffee), and their extracts, essences and concentrates
Inactive yeasts and other dead single-cell micro-organisms (excluding vaccines of heading 30.02)
Sauces and preparations therefor (soya sauce, tomato ketchup, fish sauce, chili sauce, shrimp paste, other sauces)
Mustard flour, meal and prepared mustard
Soups and broths and preparations therefor (containing or not containing meat, for infants or general use)
Homogenised composite food preparations: finely homogenised mixture of 2+ basic ingredients, retail containers not exceeding 250g, for infants/young children or dietetic purposes
Ice cream and other edible ice, whether or not containing cocoa

Food preparations not elsewhere specified or included: protein concentrates, textured protein substances, beancurd (tofu), powdered alcohol, non-dairy creamer, autolysed yeast extracts, beverage preparations, food supplements, flavoured syrups, coconut milk, tempeh, medical foods

exclusions.condition: Roasted coffee substitutes containing coffee in any proportion
Spices or other products of headings 09.04 to 09.10 (pepper, cinnamon, cloves, nutmeg, ginger, saffron, etc.)
Food preparations (OTHER than heading 21.03 or 21.04) containing more than 20% by weight of meat, fish, seafood, etc.
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 16 — Note: heading 21.03 (sauces) and 21.04 (soups/homogenised) are EXEMPT from this 20% rule

exclusions.condition: Products of heading 24.04 (tobacco substitutes / nicotine products)
Yeast put up as a medicament or other products of heading 30.03 or 30.04

classification_rules.description: Roasted coffee substitutes CONTAINING COFFEE → heading 09.01. BUT: extracts/essences/concentrates derived from those substitutes → heading 21.01 (Note 2 exception). Key: the physical form (extract vs. roasted whole) determines Chapter 21 vs Chapter 9.

For heading 21.04: 'homogenised composite food preparations' = finely homogenised mixture of TWO OR MORE basic ingredients (meat, fish, vegetables, fruit, nuts). Must be in retail containers NOT EXCEEDING 250g. Must be suitable for infants/young children or dietetic purposes. Small quantities of seasoning/preservation ingredients ignored. May contain small visible pieces.

The 20% meat/fish rule (Note 1e) that sends food preparations to Chapter 16 does NOT apply to heading 21.03 (sauces/condiments) and heading 21.04 (soups/homogenised). These two headings are explicitly excluded from the 20% rule, so fish sauce, meat soups, etc. remain in Chapter 21.

Active yeasts → 21.02.10. Inactive yeasts / dead single-cell micro-organisms → 21.02.20. Prepared baking powders → 21.02.30. HOWEVER: yeast packaged as a medicament → 30.03 or 30.04. Vaccines (even if containing micro-organisms) → 30.02.

classification_rules.rule: Coffee preparations — extract basis vs. ground roasted coffee basis
classification_rules.description: Under heading 21.01, preparations must be distinguished by their basis: (a) basis of coffee extracts/essences/concentrates → 2101.12 subheadings ending .91/.92 by sugar content; (b) basis of ground roasted coffee (paste with vegetable fat) → 2101.12.10. Instant coffee (pure extract) → 2101.11.

Heading 21.06 is a residual/catch-all for food preparations not covered elsewhere. Must verify the product is not classifiable under any other heading first. Includes: protein concentrates (2106.10), beancurd/tofu (2106.90.11-19), non-dairy creamer (2106.90.30), food supplements (2106.90.71-72), flavoured syrups (2106.90.92), coconut milk (2106.90.93), tempeh (2106.90.97), homogenised infant formulas for lactase-deficient infants (2106.90.81).

classification_rules.rule: Beverage preparations in 21.06 — alcoholic vs. non-alcoholic
classification_rules.description: Heading 21.06 covers both alcoholic and non-alcoholic preparations used for making or manufacturing beverages. Distinguish: non-alcoholic raw materials → 2106.90.53-55; alcoholic raw materials → 2106.90.61-67. Composite concentrates for simple dilution with water → 2106.90.55 (non-alc) or 2106.90.64-65 (alc).
